item.title: Oil Shock Starts to Seep Into Earnings and Stock Prices
item.description: More bitter surprises may be in store in this week’s flood of results.

item.title: US Investor Said to Pay 70% Premium for Benfica Minority Stake
item.description: US investor Entrepreneur Equity Partners has agreed to buy a minority stake in Portuguese football club Benfica at a steep premium, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Atlas Copco’s Chipmaker-Exposed Unit Shines as Orders Disappoint
item.description: Atlas Copco AB’s chipmaking-related business grew twice as strong as analysts expected, a bright spot for the company as other units disappointed.

item.title: Tullow Shares Soar as West African Oil Brings Record Price
item.description: Tullow Oil Plc’s shares surged more than 9% after tensions in the Middle East drove its West African crude to a record price.

item.title: Ping An Profit Falls as Market Decline Hurts Investment Returns
item.description: Ping An Insurance (Group) Co. said profit fell 7.4% in the first quarter, as declines in China’s stock market dented investment returns.

item.title: Maruti Profit Misses on Rising Costs, Supply Constraints
item.description: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that missed analysts’ estimates, with higher costs and supply constraints weighing on margins.

item.title: Barclays Reports £66 Billion Exposure to Non-Bank Lending
item.description: Barclays Plc laid out its £66 billion ($89.2 billion) of structured financing exposure to non-banks, becoming the latest lender to make disclosures in an area that’s unnerved investors lately.

item.title: Europe Spent €10 Billion on Iran Energy Shock Aid, Bruegel Says
item.description: European Union states have committed over €10 billion ($11.7 billion) to shield consumers and businesses as the Iran war drives up energy prices, according to the Bruegel think tank.

item.title: Hedge Fund Short Covering Fuels Demand in $5 Billion CATL Deal
item.description: Hedge funds bought the bulk of the shares in Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s $5 billion placement, with at least some of the demand driven by traders seeking to cover short positions on the battery maker’s Hong Kong-listed shares, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Norway, Sweden Are Seen Hiking Rates by Early 2027 in Survey
item.description: Economists surveyed by Bloomberg now expect both Norges Bank and the Riksbank to tighten monetary policy within the next 12 months.

item.title: Japanese Crude Supertanker Attempts Hormuz Exit in War’s First
item.description: A laden Japan-linked supertanker appears to be sailing through the Strait of Hormuz, in what may be the first attempt by an oil carrier from the country to leave the Persian Gulf since the Iran war began.

item.title: Chile Central Bank Set to Hold Rates as Policymakers Ride Out Inflation Surge
item.description: Chile’s central bank will likely hold its interest rate steady for the third straight meeting as economists dial back their growth forecasts and brace for the full brunt of the nation’s biggest fuel price hike in decades.

item.title: OpenAI-Linked Stocks Slump on Report of Startup Missing Targets
item.description: Shares in OpenAI partners such as SoftBank Group Corp. and Oracle Corp. are falling after the Wall Street Journal reported that the AI startup recently failed to meet targets for sales and new users, reviving worries about spending ahead of tech earnings.

item.title: Indian Truckers Fret as Looming Diesel Hikes Spur Panic-Buying
item.description: Alarming signs greeted motorists at some service stations in the southeast of India on Sunday: No petrol, no diesel.

item.title: Cnooc Posts Stronger Profit as Iran War Boosts Oil Prices
item.description: Cnooc Ltd. reported stronger profit in the first quarter, as China’s top offshore driller benefited from rising global crude prices from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Swiss Fund Topping 95% of Peers Sees Room for Oil Stocks to Run
item.description: A Swiss value investor who has outperformed most peers this year after sticking with his bet on energy stocks says the rally has further to run, as share prices catch up with earnings upgrades spurred by the Iran war.

item.title: Netherlands Rejects Argentina Soy Meal Cargoes Over GMO Issue
item.description: The Netherlands has rejected at least two Argentinian soybean meal cargoes this month after non-approved genetically modified material was found, threatening to disrupt a major trade flow.

item.title: Euro-Zone Banks Tighten Firms’ Credit Standards Most Since 2023
item.description: Euro-zone banks tightened corporate credit standards by the most in more than two years at the start of 2026, as geopolitical events including the Iran war added to an already prevailing trend.

item.title: ECB Says Consumer Inflation Expectations Jumped in March
item.description: Inflation expectations among euro-area consumers jumped across the board in March, a worrying sign for European Central Bank as it assesses the knock-on effects of the Iran war.

item.title: JPMorgan AM, MFS Bet on European Debt After Sharp Selloff
item.description: Investors including JPMorgan Asset Management, BlackRock Inc. and MFS Investment Management are buying shorter-term European government debt in an effort to lock in yields before they slip away.

item.title: Spain’s Jobless Rate Increases the Most Since the Pandemic
item.description: Spain’s unemployment rate saw the biggest quarterly increase since the pandemic, in a rare setback for the euro area’s best-performing major economy.

item.title: Tiger Global-Backed Spinny Said to Hire Banks for Indian IPO
item.description: Valuedrive Technologies Pvt. Ltd., which operates the Indian used-car marketplace Spinny, is weighing an initial public offering in Mumbai and has engaged investment banks for the process, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Shin-Etsu Withholds Outlook Due to Iran War Price Disruption
item.description: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co. withheld its full-year forecast, citing supply constraints and price fluctuations stemming from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: EQT Raises €3.1 Billion for New European Real Estate Fund
item.description: EQT AB has raised €3.1 billion ($3.6 billion) for its latest European real estate fund despite a challenging fundraising climate for the wider sector.

item.title: Austria Extends Bank Tax in €5.1 Billion Plan to Meet EU Budget Rules
item.description: Austria’s government coalition agreed on €5.1 billion ($6 billion) worth of budget steps, including extending a special tax on banks, as part of a two-year framework that seeks to end a European Union probe of its finances.

item.title: Thailand Plans 20% Electricity Price Cut for Low-Use Households
item.description: Thailand plans to cut electricity tariffs by about 20% for households that use relatively little power, in a bid to ease living costs amid surging global energy costs, according to Energy Minister Akanat Promphan.

item.title: Thailand Sees Weaker Growth, Higher Inflation on Mideast Crisis
item.description: Thailand expects the Middle East conflict to weaken economic growth and fuel inflation, underscoring the need for more fiscal support to shield consumers and businesses from a worsening energy crisis.

item.title: UAE Tells Some Oil Buyers to Pick Up Gulf Supply Outside Hormuz
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has informed some term customers of the availability of cargoes for loading off Fujairah, outside the Persian Gulf, as producers explore different routes to bring their oil to market.

item.title: UBS Using Party Funding to Press Swiss Lawmakers, Minister Says
item.description: Some Swiss lawmakers are afraid UBS Group AG might cut support for their parties if they speak up in support of the government’s sweeping banking reforms, according to the finance minister.

item.title: Japan Industry Group Warns of War’s Impact on Supply of Thinners
item.description: The outlook for supply of thinners and cutting oils — materials used in the production of a wide range of products from chips to cars to planes — is becoming increasingly uncertain, according to a Japanese machine tool industry group.
